tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'In colder water the basic approach is to simply cast it out and wind it with a steady retrieve'
Hackney: 'You can pop it and rip it through shallow vegetation, slow-roll it like a spinnerbait down deep, or pitch it under docks — you can fish a swim jig almost anywhere bass live'

Hackney: 'The only time I use fluorocarbon for swim jig fishing is when the water is colder and I need to fish slower and deeper — it has to do with the action of the jig on the lighter line, not the fish reacting to the line'
Hackney: 'I almost always use braid for swim jigs whether the water is gin clear or muddy. Bass aren't line shy with a moving bait'

knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'Most of the time I use a Rage Craw trailer. Sometimes I use a small swimbait if the bass are on shad. I use the standard swim jig in colder water with less cover and the heavy cover jig most other times'
Hackney: 'If you can cast and retrieve you can fish a swim jig. If you are fishing spinnerbaits now you have the ability to fish a swim jig. I always have one tied on'
